latest.title: New “Century Club” Heritage Partnership
latest.content:
26 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6166F
SEEEN PLC
26 May 2026
This is a Reach (i.e. non-regulatory) announcement and the information contained is not considered to be material or to have a significant impact on management's expectations of the Company's performance.
SEEEN plc
("SEEEN", the "Group" or the "Company")
New "Century Club" Heritage Partnership
SEEEN and Bradford Bulls ("Bulls" or the "Club") are pleased to announce a multi-year partnership focused on delivering SEEEN's new Century Club Smart Video solution to deliver an exciting new heritage sports partnership. The partnership positions the historic Rugby League club at the forefront of digital fan engagement, utilising SEEEN's interactive technology to open up its extensive archives and celebrate its legacy with supporters like never before.
The Bulls and SEEEN will collaborate to launch a premium, interactive digital heritage experience built around the Top 100 Moments in the Club's history. This innovative platform will empower Bulls fans and sponsors to engage deeply with the Club's history, offering the ability to essentially "own" a piece of their favourite iconic moments through digital collectibles, interactive video heritage activations, and associated memorabilia
As part of this launch, SEEEN will become the Club's principal partner for this Century Club collection for the five-year period.
What this means for the Club and its fans:
·
Pioneering Fan Experience:
Bradford Bulls will become the first club in Rugby League to offer a fully interactive, digital archive experience, allowing modern supporters and families to connect directly with the historic milestones that define the Club. Fans will directly help define these Top 100 moments in the Club's history, ranging from iconic moments and events from Bradford Northern era through to Bullmania and the recent return to Super League. The Club will then continue to promote these moments to fans to remind both long term and new fans of the great events that have defined the Club and brought joy to so many fans.
·
By driving fan engagement on social media, increased website traffic to vote on the moments and watch them and then driving sponsorship and relevant event and product sales, Bradford Bulls will continue to drive stronger digital outcomes which are now a key factor in ensuring the Club meets the challenging targets within Super League
Adrian Hargrave, CEO of SEEEN, commented: "Partnering with Bradford Bulls is an honour for SEEEN and we are delighted to unveil them as our first partners in rolling out our new Century Club product. The Club in its current and historic guises is one of the best known in rugby league globally and has a large and loyal fan base. We are excited to help fans celebrate some of the greatest moments in the Club history, as well as driving engagement, data and revenues for Bradford Bulls."
Jason Hirst of Bradford Bulls, added: "The return to Super League was a pivotal moment for this great Club. We have a large and very loyal fanbase and we are delighted to reward them with an opportunity to choose the greatest moments in the Club's history. The 'Century Club' will not only celebrate our greatest moments but will allow our fans and partners to connect with the club in a modern, interactive way.
For further information please contact:
SEEEN plc
Via our InvestorHub
Adrian Hargrave, Chief Executive Officer
https://investor.seeen.com/link/egG95e
Bradford Bulls
Tel: 0333 323 5075
Jason Hirst, Chief Executive Officer

latest.title: Completion of Acquisition
latest.content:
27 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 9958B
SEEEN PLC
27 April 2026
27 April 2026
SEEEN plc
("SEEEN", the "Group" or the "Company")
Completion of Acquisition
SEEEN plc (AIM: SEEN), the Smart Video technology business that optimises Key Video Moments to drive Video Commerce and transform its clients' video profitability, is pleased to announce that, further to its announcement on 21 April 2026, it has completed the acquisition of Streaming Limited, trading as MEDIAL. The Acquisition is expected to be immediately earnings accretive and accelerates SEEEN's "go-to-market" plan for education and training by developing a joint product offering creates a unique competitive advantage.
For more information, or to watch our CEO's video regarding the Acquisition and to submit questions to the management team, navigate to our investor site, where we interact directly with both existing and prospective shareholders:
https://investor.seeen.com/link/Pw7EpP
.
All terms are as defined in the announcement dated 21 April 2026, unless otherwise stated.

company_30s.what_they_do: Seeen PLC builds secure, AI‑powered video collaboration tools for businesses. Their platform lets teams record, share and analyse video in real time, adding features like live transcription, sentiment analytics and end‑to‑end encryption.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The demand for secure video is exploding as remote work and regulated industries grow. Seeen’s AI edge gives it a competitive advantage over generic conferencing apps by turning raw video into actionable insights, making it useful for compliance, training and customer support.
company_30s.current_state: After a 2019 AIM listing, the company has been on a slow‑burn growth path. Recent moves – a £1m acquisition of Medial, a new sports partnership, and a fresh investor hub – show it is expanding its product line. It still has no clear revenue stream and is burning cash, but it is actively raising capital and tightening its board.

rns_narrative: Seeen is pulling together its strategic pieces: a recent acquisition that adds AI video capabilities, a partnership that opens a sports‑media channel, and a series of board changes that streamline decision‑making.
These moves signal a shift from a shaky start‑up to a company trying to lock in a niche market and raise its profile.
The narrative is one of gradual stabilization – the company is now focused on integration and partnership, not just survival.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None – the community is still muted, so any positive news will feel like a surprise.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Cash burn, no revenue, and a history of board churn keep pessimists on edge.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has stayed flat; the lack of chatter means the market isn’t yet aware of the company’s moves.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead, which means the next piece of news could hit with maximum surprise.

near_term_catalysts: Official integration of Medial’s AI tools into Seeen’s core platform – a press release or demo that shows real‑time analytics.
First revenue announcement or a clear path to profitability in the next quarterly filing.
A partnership rollout in the sports‑media space that brings a new customer base and cash inflow.
A significant volume spike on a breakout above the 5‑day low, indicating renewed investor interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume jump of at least twice the 10‑day average and a price move that breaks the recent 5‑day support. Positive earnings or a clear roadmap to cash flow will also confirm the thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of a crash‑recovery cycle – capitulation is likely complete, but the mean‑reversion engine is still idle. A tight APEX compression suggests a sharp move could come if the catalyst materialises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has fallen well over 80% from its peak, giving it a huge upside if it rebounds to half that peak – a 50% jump from today’s level. Historically, deep‑crash signals (RED, ORANGE, GREEN) deliver 30–60% returns when they bounce, and the APEX compression points to a potential quick reversal. A 50% move would more than double the current price, offering a 2.3:1 risk‑reward profile.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Seeen still has no revenue, a high cash burn rate, and a history of board churn. If integration stalls or the partnership fails, the stock could slide deeper, wiping out the 86% drawdown. The worst‑case is a further 10% drop before the company runs out of capital.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: +50% from today’s level. Potential downside: -86% (current depth). Risk‑reward ratio ≈ 2.3:1 – a classic asymmetric play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks the 5‑day low with volume at least twice the 10‑day average. Size the position at 20% of your portfolio and set a stop 10% below the entry to cap loss. Trail the stop upward as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see either a volume spike on a breakout, a clear earnings announcement, or a partnership rollout. If none materialise within 60 days, consider tightening the stop or re‑evaluating the thesis.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has surfaced yet, so the position remains viable.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Seeen is a deep‑crash play with a clear acquisition‑driven catalyst; the lack of market chatter and tight compression set the stage for a potentially large mean‑reversion payoff.
